jokes.text: It's Chatterbox. They want me to go to the zoo and cover the birth of a baby seal.
jokes.setup: Character is in serious presidential briefing about national emergency
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted by trivial assignment to cover baby seal birth
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition between serious national crisis and absurdly trivial news assignment. Classic 30 Rock workplace comedy timing.

jokes.text: Are you familiar with the country of Gjelgjiughm? Oh, took home to gold, silver, and bronze in skeet shooting during the 2028 Olympics. - No. - Then no.
jokes.setup: President asks if character knows obscure country
jokes.punchline: Character confidently cites very specific Olympic achievement from future, then immediately admits he doesn't know it when corrected
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with specific absurd details (2028 Olympics, sweeping medals in single sport) followed by instant deflation. The unpronounceable country name adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Oh, took home to gold, silver, and bronze in skeet shooting during the 2028 Olympics. - No. - Then no.
jokes.setup: Dwayne confidently providing specific Olympic trivia
jokes.punchline: President corrects him, Dwayne immediately admits complete ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Dwayne's confident specificity immediately deflated. Great character moment showing his honesty.

jokes.text: You throw one bean in the air, two come back in your face.
jokes.setup: Explaining country has abundant coffee beans
jokes.punchline: Delivers impossibly absurd metaphor for abundance
jokes.explanation: Vivid absurd imagery that perfectly captures 30 Rock's style of taking mundane concepts to surreal extremes.

jokes.text: Have you ever seen his hands? They are soft as marshmallows, pillowy as a duchess.
jokes.setup: Prime minister insulting president in hot mic situation
jokes.punchline: Uses bizarrely specific and poetic insults about hand softness
jokes.explanation: The oddly poetic and specific nature of the insult ('pillowy as a duchess') makes this memorable character writing.

jokes.text: Also, my hands are out of frame on purpose. My hands are my best feature.
jokes.setup: Just heard his hands insulted on hot mic
jokes.punchline: Defensively claims hands are his best feature while hiding them
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing presidential vanity and defensiveness. The contradiction between claiming they're his best feature while hiding them works well.

jokes.text: Hey, is there, like, a code that wrestler's use to let each other know an injury is real? Yeah, it's... Ugh, my knee. Augh, I hurt my knee.
jokes.setup: Question about secret wrestling codes for real injuries
jokes.punchline: The 'code' is literally just saying you're hurt
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of expectation about wrestling having elaborate codes when the reality is refreshingly simple.

jokes.text: You think this is the type of situation where that saying, 'add insult to injury' comes from? Yeah, probably, Bruno. Probably.
jokes.setup: Rocky injured and being booed by fans
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's literally experiencing the origin of a common phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about literally living out an idiom. Good observational comedy that works with the wrestling context.

jokes.text: No, one of those popcorn kernels fell in my boot. - Damn. Lord ain't grantin' you no dignity today.
jokes.setup: Rocky appears to be in pain from his injured knee
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a popcorn kernel, followed by philosophical observation about his lack of dignity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of minor indignities. The philosophical 'Lord ain't grantin' you no dignity today' is perfectly timed and quotable.

jokes.text: When you're not over and you get hurt, that gives the WWF an opportunity to cut you loose. - Hey! Don't talk to him like that right now. He is delicate. - I'm not delicate. I say you are delicate; You are delicate, Dewey.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Rocky's vulnerable career position
jokes.punchline: Mother insists on treating grown wrestler as delicate despite his protests
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing overprotective mother dynamics. The authoritative 'You are delicate, Dewey' works well.

jokes.text: Pretty limited on pillow options. I'll pick you up some more. Dany doesn't believe in throw pillows. She says they just take up space.
jokes.setup: Mother offering to get more pillows for injured son
jokes.punchline: Reveals girlfriend's anti-throw pillow philosophy
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail that feels real and specific. The mundane relationship tension over decorative pillows is relatable.

jokes.text: These are all the same. Mm-mm. Check the neckline. Deep, deeper, and deepest. I told him go deepest. Popped pecks pay rent checks.
jokes.setup: Showing off t-shirt designs for wrestling gym
jokes.punchline: Reveals the only difference is increasingly deep necklines, with rhyming business philosophy
jokes.explanation: 'Popped pecks pay rent checks' is a great throwaway line that captures the character's entrepreneurial wrestling mindset.

jokes.text: She's supposed to be a silent partner. You said that, not me.
jokes.setup: Dany giving business advice as supposed silent partner
jokes.punchline: Rocky points out contradiction, Dany deflects responsibility
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor about business partnerships. The deflection is realistic but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: My only complaint is my physical therapist keeps referring to it as 'our knee.'
jokes.setup: Discussing how well rehab is going
jokes.punchline: Complains about physical therapist's possessive language about his body part
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about the weird intimacy of physical therapy. Very relatable and well-crafted.

jokes.text: Keep up the good work, Dewey. - Okay. - Au revoir.
jokes.setup: Ending business conversation with wrestler
jokes.punchline: Uses pretentious French goodbye inappropriately
jokes.explanation: The incongruous fancy French goodbye in wrestling context is mildly amusing but not standout.

jokes.text: You'll always have that criminology minor from Miami.
jokes.setup: Discussing backup career options if wrestling doesn't work out
jokes.punchline: References underwhelming academic credential as fallback
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows the limited nature of his backup plan. A criminology minor feels very specific and underwhelming.

jokes.text: Actually, sir, victim was full-grown adult male. - You're just really big.
jokes.setup: Detective assuming victim was a child based on appearance
jokes.punchline: Reveals Rock's imposing size makes normal adult look tiny
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag that plays on Rock's physicality. The misdirection and economy are excellent.

jokes.text: She ain't pregnant is she? - Dad, no. Okay, good. 'Cause she drinkin' a lot for a pregnant woman.
jokes.setup: Father asking if daughter-in-law is pregnant at wedding
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been judging her drinking based on pregnancy assumption
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing father's blunt observations. The logic is sound but inappropriately timed.

jokes.text: It's like a microwave that's also a radio. The presets are defrost, popcorn, and Cool 101.5?
jokes.setup: Describing strange wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Appliance that impossibly combines microwave and radio functions with mixed presets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd product concept. The mixing of microwave settings with radio station is perfectly weird.

jokes.text: Maybe keep it under your kitchen sink.
jokes.setup: Discussing the bizarre microwave-radio hybrid gift
jokes.punchline: Suggests hiding the obviously useless gift
jokes.explanation: Nice understated response to the absurd gift. The practical suggestion is appropriately deflating.

jokes.text: Look, I'm not gonna say I'm disappointed, but, uh, I was really expecting a roasted pig for this occasion.
jokes.setup: Being asked to comment on wedding
jokes.punchline: Immediately focuses on lack of expected pig rather than celebrating couple
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing someone's misplaced priorities at a wedding. The pig obsession is oddly specific.

jokes.text: I believe in an unbalanced portfolio. Yeah. Risk and aggression... Now that is what the Macho Man is all about, baby. Yeah, dig it.
jokes.setup: Financial advisor discussing investment strategy
jokes.punchline: Wrestler interprets financial advice through wrestling persona
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration showing how wrestler personalities bleed into normal conversations. The connection between financial risk and wrestling aggression works well.

jokes.text: Like, I purposely didn't eat pig yesterday 'cause I thought for sure that we were gonna have pig today. Yeah, Fa'amalo, Dewey and Dany. And sorry for asking for that plus one. She did not want to come. And there-there-there's no... There's no pig.
jokes.setup: Wedding guest giving congratulations
jokes.punchline: Keeps returning to pig disappointment despite trying to be polite
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the pig obsession. The character's inability to let it go despite social obligation is well-crafted.

jokes.text: So how long do we think until one of these guys splits their pants? That's why ol' Bruno always travels with a sewing kit.
jokes.setup: Watching people dance at wedding
jokes.punchline: Predicts wardrobe malfunction and reveals practical preparation
jokes.explanation: Nice observational comedy about big guys dancing. Bruno's practical preparation shows good character detail.

jokes.text: It's like I always say, Vince doesn't send a gold-plated microwave that's also a radio to wrestlers he doesn't believe in. Oh, that's what you always say, huh?
jokes.setup: Someone interpreting gift as positive sign
jokes.punchline: Called out for claiming this oddly specific wisdom is something he 'always says'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the absurd gift with nice deflation of fake wisdom. The challenge to 'always say' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Come to think of it, I owe the kid a headband.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on knowing Rocky for a long time
jokes.punchline: Random specific debt that seems completely unrelated
jokes.explanation: Perfect non sequitur. The specificity of owing a headband is wonderfully random and captures wrestling culture.

jokes.text: I guess when the invite said black tie, I forgot to mention black mustache.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Hulk Hogan's appearance at wedding
jokes.punchline: Notes the contrast between formal dress code and distinctive bleached mustache
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Hogan's iconic look clashing with formal events. Well-crafted and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'll be honest, the bleach job is hell on my nostrils. Oh, yeah. It's hell on my eyes, too.
jokes.setup: Hogan complaining about mustache bleaching effects
jokes.punchline: Rocky adds visual complaint, escalating the bodily damage
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that builds on the bleaching complaint. The mutual suffering from Hogan's look is amusing.

jokes.text: I was gonna say punchable. But, yeah, earnest is good, too.
jokes.setup: Rocky suggests 'earnest' to describe his wrestling persona
jokes.punchline: Hogan reveals he was thinking something much harsher
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. 'Punchable' is brutally honest feedback delivered with humor.

jokes.text: You got any lactate? My tummy ain't much for dairy. - I, uh... - Screw it. I'll hit the dance floor and blame Macho.
jokes.setup: Hogan asking about lactose-free options
jokes.punchline: Deciding to drink dairy anyway and blame resulting stomach issues on Macho Man
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Hogan's practical approach to party logistics and blame assignment.

jokes.text: Hey, how many do you think it'll take to pull me around the pool, two, maybe three?
jokes.setup: Discussing swimming with dolphins in Tahiti
jokes.punchline: Imagines dolphins as his personal watercraft based on his size
jokes.explanation: Good visual humor playing on Rock's size relative to dolphins. The casual planning of animal exploitation is amusingly oblivious.

jokes.text: Don't use it. It's been recalled. Cancer stuff. Terrible product. I'm sending you a blender instead.
jokes.setup: Rocky thanking Vince for the bizarre microwave-radio wedding gift
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals gift is dangerously defective
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that escalates the absurd gift into actual danger. The casual delivery of 'cancer stuff' is darkly hilarious.

jokes.text: That's so weird. Biloxi was my second choice for honeymoon destinations.
jokes.setup: Learning his first wrestling assignment back is in Biloxi
jokes.punchline: Reveals the industrial town was somehow a honeymoon consideration
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that shows Rocky's practical/unromantic side. Biloxi as honeymoon destination is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I like a guy with the confidence to wear pink pants.
jokes.setup: Discussing wrestling factions
jokes.punchline: Appreciates wrestler's bold fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Nice observational comment about wrestling fashion. The appreciation for confidence in questionable choices is amusing.

jokes.text: Wait, that was your 'bad dude' look? Are those denim shorts?
jokes.setup: Rocky describing his transformation to bad guy character
jokes.punchline: Incredulous reaction to his supposedly intimidating outfit
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Rocky's tough guy image. Denim shorts as intimidating wear is perfectly absurd for wrestling context.

jokes.text: Orville Redenbacher you are not.
jokes.setup: Continuing the 'no pop' theme
jokes.punchline: Popcorn reference extending the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the pop metaphor with specific popcorn reference. Well-crafted escalation.

jokes.text: I'ma always shoot you straight. You ain't pop worth a damn. - Straight out the gate, huh? Snap, crackle, no pop. - And you're still going. Orville Redenbacher you are not.
jokes.setup: Rocky asking if he got any crowd reaction
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate cereal-themed ways of saying he got no reaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay around 'pop' meaning crowd reaction and cereal sounds. The Orville Redenbacher reference is perfect escalation.

jokes.text: People ain't gonna give a damn till you make them give a damn.
jokes.setup: Dwayne's frustration about fan reaction
jokes.punchline: Philosophical wrestling wisdom
jokes.explanation: Great character moment with genuine wrestling wisdom that becomes motivational. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You're the fourth man in a four-man crew. I don't think Vince is thinking about your angles.
jokes.setup: Rocky asking about his character motivation and story angles
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of his unimportant position
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of harsh reality. The mathematical precision ('fourth man in a four-man crew') makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Boss, I got my time card here for you to sign. I worked one hour on Monday. Two hours on Tuesday. Wednesday was a bank holiday. Four hours on...
jokes.setup: Trying to have serious conversation with wrestling boss
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by mundane employee time card accounting
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the mundane business side interrupting dramatic wrestling storylines. The bank holiday detail is nice.

jokes.text: 'Raw' is like a fine-tuned Swiss watch, hmm? Timed to the second. Or better yet, like a sausage that's been stuffed full of pork to the brink of explosion.
jokes.setup: Explaining why there's no extra TV time available
jokes.punchline: Shifts from elegant watch metaphor to grotesque sausage imagery
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from refined to crude metaphors. The vivid sausage imagery is perfectly bizarre and memorable. Great character voice.

jokes.text: Rocky, pull up your tank. Your son's wife can see your chi-chis. Everybody can see 'em. I worked hard on these babies. Ba-boom!
jokes.setup: Mother telling son to cover up at wrestling event
jokes.punchline: Rocky proudly shows off his physique instead
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic with mother's embarrassment vs son's pride. The 'chi-chis' and 'ba-boom' are appropriately silly.

jokes.text: My cat throws up a human thumb. Is it interesting? Sure. But is it good? No. - Did that really happen? - Hey, stay focused.
jokes.setup: Making analogy about interesting vs good performance
jokes.punchline: Uses bizarrely specific and disturbing example, gets sidetracked by its reality
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd analogy that's so specific and disturbing it becomes the focus. The immediate distraction from the point is perfect.

jokes.text: She uses your catchphrases in her speeches. She knows all of your matches in detail, and I think, with the exception of 'Huskies,' has seen all of your movies. Is she not an animation fan or something? Because 'Huskies' was good.
jokes.setup: Explaining prime minister is a big Rock fan
jokes.punchline: Rock's defensive reaction to one movie being excluded
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rock's sensitivity about his filmography. The defensive 'Huskies was good' is appropriately petty.

jokes.text: Will you go on a coffee run for America? I'm honored, Mr. President. Thank you. But no. - Fantastic. Wait... Wait, I didn't hear that last bit.
jokes.setup: President asking Rock to take on diplomatic mission
jokes.punchline: Rock politely declines, President mishears and celebrates before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat with great timing. The President's automatic celebration followed by confusion is expertly structured.

jokes.text: I'm honored, Mr. President. Thank you. But no. - Fantastic. Wait... Wait, I didn't hear that last bit.
jokes.setup: President expecting acceptance of mission
jokes.punchline: Dwayne declining, President's delayed processing of rejection
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect timing. The President's processing delay is well-crafted and ends the episode strongly.
